display_name: show tech-support is collected section-by-section over time — its counters are not a single atomic snapshot
context: Correlating counters or state across different sections of a single show tech-support capture
observation: Two sections of the same show tech disagree, or a counter in one section seems inconsistent with another section
naive_interpretation: The device state is inconsistent or corrupt because two parts of the same capture do not line up
correct_interpretation: show tech-support runs many individual show commands in sequence; a large capture can take seconds to minutes to complete. Each section is a snapshot from the moment that command ran, not a single point in time. Counters that incremented between sections, or a neighbor that changed state partway through collection, will make sections look inconsistent. This is expected — the capture is a time-spread collection, not an atomic dump. Note the timestamps and prefer live, simultaneous commands when correlating fast-moving counters.
why: show tech-support is a scripted concatenation of independent show commands executed back-to-back, not a frozen state snapshot
On a busy or large device the run spans a meaningful interval, during which counters and states keep changing
Each section reflects the instant its underlying command executed, so cross-section comparisons of live counters can legitimately differ
Apparent inconsistency is a timing artifact of sequential collection, not data corruption

confirmation_commands.purpose: Anchor capture timing; compare against per-section timestamps to gauge how spread out the collection was
Re-read a specific counter live to confirm whether a cross-section discrepancy is just elapsed time

traps: Treating two sections of one show tech as simultaneous and concluding the device state is inconsistent
Correlating fast-moving counters across sections instead of re-reading them live together
Ignoring per-section timestamps that reveal how much time elapsed during collection
Drawing root-cause conclusions from a stale section captured minutes before the section it is compared against
